skill.name: qa
skill.description: Runs an interactive QA session where users describe bugs, the agent asks limited clarifying questions, explores repository context, and files GitHub issues.
skill.summary: Turn conversational QA reports into concise GitHub issues with repository-aware context.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The confirmed risk is an instruction to run the GitHub CLI to create issues without asking for pre-review. The static weak cryptography detections are false positives in prose and issue templates, and the other backtick detections are Markdown formatting rather than shell execution.

medium_findings.title: GitHub Issue Creation Without Pre-Review
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create issues with gh issue create and not ask the user to review first. This is a legitimate QA workflow, but it can create GitHub issues containing incorrect or sensitive content if repository permissions and user intent are not confirmed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a direct instruction to run an external GitHub CLI command with a durable side effect. The intent is workflow automation rather than malware, so medium risk is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm detections are false positives. The cited lines contain QA instructions, expected behavior text, or issue template wording, not cryptographic APIs, hashing algorithms, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found plain-language QA instructions only. No cryptographic code or configuration is present in the single scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Static Matches
low_findings.description: The backtick detections at the issue template blocks are false positives. They mark Markdown fences around issue body examples and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are Markdown code fences used to show issue templates. They are not Ruby shell backticks or executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External GitHub CLI Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the agent to use gh issue create, which can modify a GitHub repository by opening new issues. This is expected for the skill, but it should be disclosed because it performs an external side effect.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly named on the cited line and has a clear external side effect. No evidence of command injection, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Bug reports often arrive as incomplete conversations that are hard to turn into useful work. This skill guides a focused QA session and files concise GitHub issues using project language.
actual_capabilities: Asks a small number of clarifying questions about expected and actual behavior.
Explores repository context to learn domain language before writing issues.
Decides whether a report should become one issue or several smaller issues.
Creates GitHub issues in dependency order when a breakdown is needed.
Writes issue bodies that emphasize user-visible behavior and reproduction steps.
Shares created issue URLs and summarizes blocking relationships.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI access and repository permissions to create issues.
Does not implement fixes or validate root causes.
May file issues before the user reviews the final wording.
Depends on the user's description for accurate reproduction steps.

use_cases.title: Convert Support Reports
use_cases.description: Turn a user complaint into a clear issue with expected behavior, actual behavior, and reproduction steps.

use_cases.title: Triage QA Sessions
use_cases.description: Run a focused testing session and create separate issues for independent defects.

use_cases.title: Prepare Developer Work
use_cases.description: Capture bug reports in domain language so developers can understand and verify the requested work.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic QA
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a simple bug report intake.
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a QA session. I will describe one bug, and I want you to ask only the questions needed to file a useful GitHub issue.

prompt_templates.title: File One Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a single issue from a known problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me file this bug as one GitHub issue: the save button reports success, but the changes are missing after refresh.

prompt_templates.title: Break Down Multiple Problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Separate a broad report into independent work items.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run QA intake for this report and split it into separate GitHub issues only where the defects can be fixed independently.

prompt_templates.title: Use Repository Language
prompt_templates.scenario: Create issues that match project terminology.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explore the repository context while we discuss this QA report, then file issues using the product terms developers already use.

output_examples.input: The checkout form says payment failed, but the order is still created.
output_examples.output: A GitHub issue describing the checkout inconsistency, expected behavior, reproduction steps, and user-facing impact.

output_examples.input: The onboarding flow has three unrelated problems across validation, confirmation text, and redirect behavior.
output: A tracking summary that separates each behavior problem.
Individual issue URLs ordered by dependency.
A short note showing which issues are blocked and which can start immediately.

best_practices: Confirm repository and GitHub permissions before starting the session.
Provide concrete reproduction details, inputs, and observed behavior.
Review created issue URLs promptly for sensitive or inaccurate details.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for private reports that must not leave the chat.
Do not ask it to diagnose or fix the code during QA intake.
Do not combine unrelated defects into one broad issue.

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to create GitHub issues with gh issue create when the environment has access.

faq.question: Will it ask many follow-up questions?
faq.answer: No. The instructions limit clarification to a small number of focused questions.

faq.question: Does it include file paths in issues?
faq.answer: No. The skill tells the agent to write durable issues without file paths or line numbers.

faq.question: Can it split one report into several issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It separates independent symptoms or failure modes into smaller issues when appropriate.

faq.question: Does it fix the reported bugs?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on QA intake, context gathering, and issue creation.

faq.question: What should users check before using it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the target repository, GitHub CLI authentication, and whether reported details may be public.
